steps.title: Find the length of the rectangle
steps.content:
We're given that the width is 9 centimeters. The length is 50 centimeters longer than the width, so:
$$\text{Length} = 9 + 50 = 59 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Calculate the area using the rectangle formula
steps.content:
Area of a rectangle is width times length:
$$\text{Area} = 9 \times 59$$
$$= 9 \times (60 - 1) = 540 - 9 = 531 \text{ square centimeters}$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
